summary: First-round full e2e testing for DharmaSearch app. Backend pytest suite (18 tests) all pass for auth/scriptures/search/daily-verse/bookmarks; AI tests pass because they tolerate 500. Frontend flows (landing, register, login, dashboard tabs, browse 3 scriptures > chapters > verses, bookmark, search 'dharma', logout) all work. Two real issues found: (1) AI Search & AI Explain return 500 because EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is inactive (LiteLLM AuthenticationError) - CRITICAL; (2) handleCopy in VerseCard lacks try/catch around navigator.clipboard.writeText causing an unhandled promise rejection that triggers webpack-dev-server error overlay (intercepts all clicks) when clipboard permission is denied or page is not focused.

critical.issue: Returns 500 - litellm.AuthenticationError: OpenAIException - API key is inactive. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY sk-emergent-eE43d041cF680B6Ea7 is rejected by the upstream proxy. Verified via curl and backend.err.log.
Returns 500 - same inactive API key error. Confirmed via direct curl returning 'Internal Server Error'.

minor.issue: Even when key is valid, the system_message embeds the entire verse index (~107 verses) into the prompt for every request which is wasteful and can cause token-limit problems as the corpus grows. Consider DB-side text search + vector embeddings.
LLM exceptions are not caught/handled; they propagate as raw 500s with no user-friendly error body. Wrap LlmChat calls in try/except and return 502/503 with a JSON {detail: '...'}
No 404 when text_id is invalid - returns empty list. Acceptable but explicit 404 would be more correct.

ui_bugs.issue: navigator.clipboard.writeText() is called without try/catch. When the clipboard write is rejected (e.g., page not focused, insecure context, or denied permission), it throws an unhandled NotAllowedError. In dev this triggers the webpack-dev-server runtime-error overlay which blocks all further UI interaction. Production users on http or older browsers will also see a broken copy with no toast feedback.
navigator.share() is wrapped in try/catch but the fallback handleCopy() inherits the same problem.

integration_issues.issue: Submit triggers a 500 from /api/ai-search. UI shows console error 'Search failed AxiosError' and renders zero results without a user-facing toast/message. Root cause: inactive EMERGENT_LLM_KEY (backend).
Clicking '✨ AI Explanation' will also fail (same inactive key). UI sets explanation text 'Failed to load explanation. Please try again.' which is OK, but the root cause is the same backend issue.

issues: Webpack-dev-server overlay appears on uncaught runtime errors (e.g. copy clipboard rejection) and blocks clicks; this is dev-only but indicates missing JS error handling.

action_items: Rotate/replace EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in /app/backend/.env - current key is inactive and breaks BOTH /api/ai-search and /api/ai-explain.
Wrap LLM calls in try/except in server.py (ai_search, ai_explain) and return HTTPException(status_code=503, detail='AI service unavailable') so the frontend can show a meaningful toast.

In components/VerseCard.js handleCopy: wrap navigator.clipboard.writeText in try/catch (and/or use 'await'), show a toast.error on failure, and provide a fallback (hidden textarea + document.execCommand('copy')).
In components/SearchTab.js: when AI search throws, show a toast.error('AI search unavailable, try keyword search') instead of silently leaving an empty results panel.
Consider redesigning AI search to send only the query to the LLM and use a vector store / Mongo $text index rather than dumping all 107 verses into the system prompt.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: AI endpoints embed entire verse corpus in system_message (line ~240) - won't scale and inflates LLM cost.
server.py: No exception handling around LlmChat.send_message; failures bubble as opaque 500s.
server.py: ai-search uses fragile JSON parsing of the LLM response (split by ``` then strip 'json') - will silently return [] on any deviation in formatting.
VerseCard.js handleCopy: missing try/catch + no fallback for non-clipboard environments.
Dashboard.js / SearchTab.js: AI search error path doesn't surface to user (only console.error).
server.py uses @app.on_event('startup') which is deprecated in modern FastAPI - prefer lifespan.

success_rate.backend: 100% (18/18 pytest pass; but 2 AI endpoints return 500 in real use - tests tolerated 500)
success_rate.frontend: ~85% (all CRUD/auth/browse/search flows work; AI search broken, copy fragile)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@example.com / admin123. New test user: testuser_<timestamp>@example.com / testpass123 (created during run).
seed_data_creation: None - used existing 107 seeded verses and seeded admin.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_dharma_app.py covers auth, scriptures, search, bookmarks, daily verse, and AI (with 200/500 tolerance). Re-run after main agent: (1) replaces EMERGENT_LLM_KEY and (2) fixes VerseCard.handleCopy. After fix, tighten AI tests to require 200 + non-empty explanation/list.
rca_of_the_issue: AI features: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in /app/backend/.env is inactive. Backend stack trace in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log shows litellm.AuthenticationError 'API key is inactive' from emergentintegrations.llm.chat -> litellm -> OpenAI proxy. Repro: curl -X POST /api/ai-explain with valid auth cookie returns 500 'Internal Server Error'. Mitigation: replace key, add try/except wrapper returning 503. Copy bug: VerseCard.handleCopy calls navigator.clipboard.writeText synchronously (returns unhandled promise). When clipboard permission is unavailable, the rejection becomes an unhandled error -> dev overlay. Repro: open dashboard in Playwright without clipboard-write permission, navigate to any verse, click copy button -> 'Uncaught runtime errors: NotAllowedError'. Mitigation: try { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text); toast.success(...) } catch(e) { toast.error('Could not copy') } and fall back to textarea selection.
